Frederiks Kirke
Reason: Known as The Marble Church for its rococo architecture, Frederiks Kirke is an iconic symbol of Copenhagen's architectural heritage.
Price: $20
Opening days: Tuesday to Friday

Location: The Little Mermaid Statue
Reason: This statue is a character from Grimm's Fairy Tales and is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Copenhagen.
Price: Free
Opening days: All week

Location: Noma
Reason: Noma is a world-famous restaurant known for its culinary masterpieces. A dinner here is a must for any food lover.

Opening days: All week

Reason: The Round Tower is a 17th-century tower located in central Copenhagen. It was built as an astronomical observatory.
Price: DKK 40
Opening days: All week

Reason: It is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.

Amarienborg is the winter home of the Danish royal family. It's a must-visit due to its historical significance and the change of guard that happens at 12:00 every day.

Copenhagen follows left-hand drive. Be careful if you plan to drive or rent a car.
Copenhagen follows left-hand drive. Be careful if you plan to drive or rent a car.
The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius. Make sure to pack warm clothes if you're visiting in winter.

There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.

The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so make sure to exchange your money.
